name: GX-Pack R1: Fix Measurement
description: Replace naive dirty_score with 12-signal multi-dimensional health vector. Fixes G3 (naive scoring) and G6 (blind monitoring). Foundation for all other remediation phases.
qualityGates: All 12 signal scripts produce valid JSON with score 0-100
Health combinator produces composite weighted score
gx-mid-guard.sh uses new health model (old dirty_score removed)
Integration verification script passes all checks
User explicitly approves R1 gate with evidence

userStories.title: Health metrics schema + state file
userStories.description: Design the 12-signal health vector schema and create initial state file
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 signal keys present in signals object
userStories.notes: Foundation story — all signal scripts depend on this schema

userStories.title: Signal: Plan adherence (S1)
userStories.description: Script that scores how well agent actions align with current plan node scope
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-flight check for jq (exits gracefully if missing)
Handles missing state files (returns score 100 with reason no_data)
Output is valid JSON with signal, score, detail fields
Formula documented in output JSON detail
userStories.notes: CR-13 S1, CR-01 hard block source. Uses enforcement.json violations / turnCount

userStories.title: Signal: Hierarchy freshness (S2)
userStories.description: Script that scores how recently hierarchy.json was updated, with level-aware decay
acceptanceCriteria: Differentiates L2 (decay 10/min) vs L3 (decay 5/min) per CR-14
Score decays with time since last hierarchy update
userStories.notes: CR-13 S2, CR-03 staleness core signal

userStories.title: Signal: Decision velocity (S3)
userStories.description: Score based on ratio of decisions logged vs turns elapsed
acceptanceCriteria: Reads decisions.jsonl if exists, score 50 if not (neutral)
Score = normalized ratio of decision_count / turn_count
Output valid JSON with formula in detail
userStories.notes: Depends on decisions.jsonl existing (R2 creates it). Score 50 (neutral) until R2.

userStories.title: Signal: TODO progression (S4)
userStories.description: Score based on task completion rate over recent turns
acceptanceCriteria: Calculates moving average of completions per turn

userStories.title: Signal: Context saturation (S5)
userStories.description: Score based on estimated context window usage
acceptanceCriteria: Estimates token count from enforcement.json turnCount
Score 100 = fresh context, decays toward 0 as tokens grow

userStories.title: Signal: HARD STOP compliance (S6)
userStories.description: Score based on whether HARD STOP exists and is respected
acceptanceCriteria: Reads todo.json for HARD STOP item
Score 100 = HARD STOP exists and no tasks after it are in_progress
Score 0 = HARD STOP violated (tasks after it being worked)

userStories.title: Signal: Delegation efficiency (S7)
userStories.description: Score based on useful sub-task completions vs total delegations
acceptanceCriteria: Score = completions / delegations ratio normalized to 0-100

userStories.title: Signal: Scope proximity (S8)
userStories.description: Score based on near-miss scope violations
acceptanceCriteria: Score 100 = no near-misses, decays with proximity to boundary

userStories.title: Signal: Domain continuity (S9)
userStories.description: Score based on tool call domains matching declared intent
acceptanceCriteria: Reads runtime-profile.json intent and enforcement tool data
Score = % of actions consistent with intent

userStories.title: Signal: Evidence quality (S10)
userStories.description: Score based on % of completed tasks with evidence field populated

userStories.title: Signal: Turn normalized (S11)
userStories.description: Score based on position in expected session lifecycle
acceptanceCriteria: Score 100 at start, decays toward 0 at expected session end (100 turns)

userStories.title: Signal: Chain integrity (S12)
userStories.description: Score based on operational vs degraded chains. Makes stubs VISIBLE.
acceptanceCriteria: Checks each chain's scripts: stub vs full implementation
Output includes per-chain status: operational or DEGRADED
Stub chains report CHAIN_DEGRADED not healthy (CR-11)
userStories.notes: This is what makes stubs visible per CR-11

userStories.title: Signal combinator (gx-health-compute.sh)
userStories.description: Runs all 12 signals, combines via configurable weights, computes velocity, persists
acceptanceCriteria: Script runs all 12 signal scripts from signals/ directory
Handles individual signal failures gracefully (default score 50)
Computes velocity per signal (diff from previous health-metrics.json)
Output includes per-signal scores AND composite
userStories.notes: The core combinator. All signal scripts must exist first.

userStories.title: Replace gx-mid-guard.sh with new health model
userStories.description: Mid-guard becomes thin wrapper calling gx-health-compute.sh
acceptanceCriteria: Old dirty_score logic fully removed from gx-mid-guard.sh
Mid-guard calls gx-health-compute.sh and formats output
All existing consumers (context-injection.ts, commands) still work
New output includes all 12 signal scores
Spec-vs-impl mismatch fixed (no undocumented drift < 40 clause)

userStories.title: Replace computeDrift() in context-injection.ts
userStories.description: Context injection reads health-metrics.json instead of computing inline
acceptanceCriteria: Context injection shows per-signal breakdown (not just one number)

userStories.title: R1 Integration verification + phase gate
userStories.description: Run full verification, present evidence to user for approval
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 signal scripts produce valid JSON with score 0-100
userStories.notes: HARD STOP: present evidence to user, wait for approval before R2
